raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":1.0,"R1.3":1.0,"R1.4":0.0,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.0,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should the help focus mainly on substantive argument and claims, or mainly on prose polish and readability?","rationale":{"R1.1":"The section includes thesis-level interpretations about ambiguity signals and whether selective response is solved, so conceptual framing is centrally implicated.","R1.2":"The delegation requires checking whether the metric definition, reported computation, and conclusions cohere, because contradictions would invalidate the section.","R1.3":"The section makes empirical claims from reported model scores and subsets, so claims must align with the table-like results described.","R1.5":"Because the request targets an entire section, section-level ordering and narrative arc are central expected responsibilities.","R1.6":"A general request for help with a section normally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, and concision.","RX.1":"Any projected assistance should disclose uncertainty about intended scope because the delegation is underspecified.","RX.2":"The section contains strong conclusions, so avoiding overclaiming is an always-active responsibility.","RX.3":"The agent must stay within helping with this section rather than evaluating unrelated parts of the paper.","RX.4":"Downstream-harm avoidance applies to preventing misleading empirical or methodological claims from being strengthened uncritically.","RX.5":"Any intervention should preserve traceability to the artifact’s reported metrics, model list, and stated benchmark setup."}}

parsed.clarification_question: Should the help focus mainly on substantive argument and claims, or mainly on prose polish and readability?

rationale.R1.1: The section includes thesis-level interpretations about ambiguity signals and whether selective response is solved, so conceptual framing is centrally implicated.
rationale.R1.2: The delegation requires checking whether the metric definition, reported computation, and conclusions cohere, because contradictions would invalidate the section.
rationale.R1.3: The section makes empirical claims from reported model scores and subsets, so claims must align with the table-like results described.
rationale.R1.5: Because the request targets an entire section, section-level ordering and narrative arc are central expected responsibilities.
rationale.R1.6: A general request for help with a section normally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, and concision.
rationale.RX.1: Any projected assistance should disclose uncertainty about intended scope because the delegation is underspecified.
rationale.RX.2: The section contains strong conclusions, so avoiding overclaiming is an always-active responsibility.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within helping with this section rather than evaluating unrelated parts of the paper.
rationale.RX.4: Downstream-harm avoidance applies to preventing misleading empirical or methodological claims from being strengthened uncritically.
rationale.RX.5: Any intervention should preserve traceability to the artifact’s reported metrics, model list, and stated benchmark setup.
